prompt:
Motion simulator regression from the gantry test bench. A 400 ms tick at 2500 mm/s² from rest should finish at 0.20 m and 1.00 m/s, but both the C ABI and the C++ adapter report no movement. Longer ticks can move, but fractional accelerations are still low: 2000 ms at 750 mm/s² behaves as though the acceleration were zero. The generated binding names and units match the controller documentation.

The repository contains a generated C binding, a handwritten adapter header, the ABI wrapper, and the simulation core. Please trace a tick through those layers and repair the physical calculation at its owner. ABI-facing structs, function signatures, status codes, and adapter mappings are compatibility contracts. Prediction must remain non-mutating, null-pointer errors must remain errors, signed braking inputs must work, and direct core callers must agree with ABI callers. Do not patch expected values into a wrapper or change the wire units.

Acceptance is `make test`. The generated binding, adapter, Makefile, harness, and acceptance test are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: distinguish generated ABI bindings and adapter glue from the C++ simulation core that owns unit conversion and motion integration
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits 1 because a 400 millisecond direct-core tick at 2500 millimeters per second squared leaves position and velocity at zero
full_distill.success_contract: direct core, adapter, advance, and prediction paths produce the same SI motion for fractional positive and negative inputs while ABI layouts, signatures, errors, and non-mutating prediction remain unchanged
adversarial_factors: direct SimulationCore tests bypass the ABI wrapper, so scaling or hard-coded corrections in generated-facing glue do not repair the owner
sub-second durations and non-whole signed accelerations are tested independently, so fixing only one conversion or only positive motion remains wrong
the protected generated header and adapter are compiled with static signature and field-type assertions, rejecting ABI changes and moving conversion into the wire seam
prediction and advance are compared from the same initial state while null calls are checked, rejecting disabled mutation or broad error suppression
sequential ticks with values absent from the incident example reject a fixture-specific output table
